Här blir du en central del av Metos Sverige, ett engagerat team som levererar smarta, hållbara och effektiva lösningar.
   

  

**Om rollen**
 Som säljare hos Metos blir du en central del av vårt svenska säljteam. Du arbetar nära övriga säljare samt våra Key Account Managers och kedjekundansvariga, initialt nationellt och på sikt även internationellt. Rollen präglas av hög frihet, stort ansvar och ett tydligt mål: att stärka Metos marknadsposition och öka försäljningen i Storstockholm och Mälardalen.
   

  

 Det är en bred och varierad roll där du arbetar både med nykundsbearbetning och vidareutveckling av befintliga kunder. Du arbetar med att sälja allt från enstaka produkter till att projektleda större affärer kring kompletta kökslösningar genom hela verksamhetens livscykel. Alla kundrelationer är viktiga och följs alltid upp.
   

  

 Du arbetar mot restauranger, caféer, privata skolor, kedjor, dagligvaruhandel, bensinstationer, barer och andra verksamheter där det finns behov av professionella kökslösningar.
   

  

 Du utgår från vårt huvudkontor i Sätra, Stockholm och ditt distrikt omfattar Storstockholm och Mälardalen.
   

  

**Dina arbetsuppgifter**
 I rollen som säljare bygger och utvecklar du långsiktiga kundrelationer och driver självständigt hela säljprocessen, från behovsanalys till avslut och vidare partnerskap.
   

  

* Ansvara för kundrelationen hela vägen och skapa långsiktiga samarbeten
* Identifiera behov och erbjuda rätt helhetslösningar
* Representera Metos på mässor, branschevent och kundaktiviteter
* Förhandla och slutföra affärsavtal
* Samarbeta med produktutveckling, kundtjänst och logistik för att säkerställa hög kvalitet i leveransen
* Rapportera säljaktiviteter och resultat löpande
* Samarbeta med kollegor inom Metos Sverige och Metos Group


**Vad har Richard Hunter från vår säljorganisation att säga om tjänsten och företaget?**
*"Det bästa med att jobba på Metos är att försäljningen är ett resultat av relationen du bygger med kunden, inte tvärtom. Det är inte kvot och tryck som driver oss, det är förståelse. Vi förstår kundens verksamhet, vi hjälper dem möta sina utmaningar, och affären kommer naturligt ur det. Den röda tråden på Metos är att vi genuint gillar kundkontakt och långsiktiga relationer, och att man är beredd att gå den extra milen när kunden behöver det.*
*För att trivas i rollen som säljare behöver du gilla att bygga relationer på riktigt och är genuint nyfiken på kundens vardag. Du behöver inte komma från branschen, men du behöver vilja förstå den. Prestigelöshet och ett lösningsorienterat sätt är viktiga egenskaper här. Ingen förväntas kunna allt, ibland är den bästa lösningen att be om hjälp, och det är helt okej hos oss. Gör du det så finns det väldigt goda förutsättningar att lyckas här."*
**Vem är du?**
 Vi söker en energisk och affärsdriven säljare som är trygg i kunddialogen och trivs med att skapa nya affärer.
   

  

 Du har:
   

  

* Erfarenhet av B2B-försäljning, gärna inom livsmedel, restauranggrossist, foodservice eller liknande
* Förmåga att förstå kundens behov och skapa värdebaserade lösningar
* God förståelse för kundernas vardag (t.ex. inköp, drift och logistik)
* Vana att arbeta självständigt mot mål och budget
* Förmåga att hantera flera parallella kontakter och projekt
* God systemvana och lätt för digitala verktyg
* B-körkort
* Goda kunskaper i svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift


**Personliga egenskaper**
* Affärsdriven med fokus på resultat och lönsamhet
* Relationsskapande och förtroendeingivande
* Självständig men samtidigt en lagspelare
* Orädd och initiativtagande
* Strukturerad med starkt driv
* Flexibel och lyhörd


**Vad erbjuder vi?**
 Hos Metos får du en viktig roll i en bransch med stor betydelse i samhället. Du blir en del av ett starkt och etablerat varumärke i en internationell koncern med stora utvecklingsmöjligheter, både nationellt och internationellt.
   

  

* En utvecklande roll med stor påverkan i din egen vardag
* Möjlighet att växa inom en internationell organisation i stark tillväxt
* Attraktiva förmåner, kollektivavtal och en trygg arbetsmiljö
* Tillgång till Metos-koncernens samlade kompetens och nätverk i Europa


 Metos är en del av en internationell koncern med verksamhet i flera europeiska länder och stark global förankring. Här får du möjlighet att utvecklas tillsammans med engagerade kollegor i en miljö där initiativ och engagemang verkligen uppskattas.
   

  

**Kontakt och ansökan**
 I denna rekrytering samarbetar vi med Säljpoolens rekryteringskonsult Josefine Planander. Du når Josefine Planander på 031-7045173.
   

  

 Skicka in din ansökan via ansökningsknappen. Vi arbetar med löpande urval under denna rekrytering, vi vill därför gärna ha in din ansökan snarast möjligt.
   

  

**Välkommen med din ansökan!**
**Om Metos**
*Metos är en finsk koncern som tillverkar och marknadsför utrustning för professionella kök. Metos är marknadsledare i Norden, Baltikum och Beneluxländerna och tillhör ALI Group, vilket gör oss till en av världens största leverantörer av storköksutrustning. Metos Storkök AB är ett helägt dotterbolag, ett säljbolag med 300 milj i omsättning och ca 60 anställda med kontor i Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Falun, Örebro, Ängelholm och en rikstäckande serviceorganisation på över 40 orter i Sverige.*

title: V.I.E. Innovation and Intrapreneurship
description:
Req ID: 518722
   

  

 At Alstom, we understand transport networks and what moves people. From high-speed trains, metros, monorails, and trams, to turnkey systems, services, infrastructure, signalling and digital mobility, we offer our diverse customers the broadest portfolio in the industry. Every day, more than 80 000 colleagues lead the way to greener and smarter mobility worldwide, connecting cities as we reduce carbon and replace cars.
   

  

 Type of contract: Volontariat International en Entreprise (VIE). To learn more about the VIE program and the eligibility requirements, please, Mon Volontariat International
   

  

 Could you be the full-time Innovation Programme Leader in Sweden we’re looking for?
   

  

**Your future role**
 Take on a new challenge and apply your engineering and project management expertise in a cutting-edge field. You’ll work alongside dynamic and forward-thinking teammates.
   

  

 You'll play a pivotal role in driving innovation and sustainability initiatives, ensuring the successful delivery of high-priority projects. Day-to-day, you’ll work closely with teams across the business (R&D, engineering, and external innovation partners), coordinate technical activities, and manage stakeholder relationships, among other responsibilities.
   

  

 You’ll specifically take care of leading advanced manufacturing and eco-material projects, but also ensuring alignment with Alstom's long-term sustainability and innovation goals.
   

  

 We’ll look to you for:
   

  

* Driving use cases through the Acceleration phase in line with Alstom’s Collaborative Innovation Programme
* Defining and managing project scope, milestones, deliverables, risks, and dependencies
* Coordinating engineering activities such as mechanical design, structural simulations, and benchmarking
* Ensuring compliance with railway engineering constraints while leveraging innovative manufacturing capabilities
* Acting as the operational interface with start-ups and internal contributors
* Preparing and animating technical workshops, design reviews, and steering points
* Providing clear status reporting and supporting go/no-go decisions


 Ensuring documentation, confidentiality, and IP awareness in line with contractual requirements
   

  

**All About You**
 We value passion and attitude over experience. That’s why we don’t expect you to have every single skill. Instead, we’ve listed some that we think will help you succeed and grow in this role:
   

  

* Master’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Engineering, Materials, or equivalent
* Solid understanding of structural mechanics, manufacturing processes, and product development
* Experience or understanding of technical or engineering project management in an industrial or R&D environment
* Knowledge of innovation acceleration, prototype development, or pre-industrial processes
* Familiarity with railway, automotive, aerospace, or heavy industries
* Strong stakeholder management and communication skills
* Structured, autonomous, and delivery-focused mindset


 You don’t need to be a train enthusiast to thrive with us. We guarantee that when you step onto one of our trains with your friends or family, you’ll be proud. If you’re up for the challenge, we’d love to hear from you!
   

  

**Important to note**
 As a global business, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that celebrates diversity across the 63 countries we operate in. We’re committed to creating an inclusive workplace for everyone.

title: Analyst
description:
**The opportunity: Analyst, Institutional Clients Coverage – Nordics**
 Being part of HSBC in Sweden, you’ll help evolve and grow our Institutional Clients business across the Nordic region. The role is based in Sweden and reports directly to the Head of Institutional Client Group, Nordic region. You’ll work closely with Senior Bankers and Global Relationship Bankers to support coverage of leading Nordic institutional clients, leveraging HSBC’s international network.
   

  

**What You Will Do**
 As an Analyst, you’ll strengthen your fundamentals of banking including relationship management, sales and solutions, credit analysis and customer due diligence. You’ll gain exposure to HSBC’s broad product offering and support senior bankers in the origination and execution of solutions across:
   

  

* Debt Capital Markets (DCM)
* Acquisition financing and structured finance
* Cash management (Global Payment Solutions) and trade finance (Global Trade Solutions)
* Custody / Securities Services
* Global Markets across asset classes (FX, Rates, Credit, Equities and Derivatives)
* Asset management


 The client base will be Nordic institutional clients, including banks, asset managers, insurance companies, fintechs, public sector/sovereign-related entities and financial sponsors.
   

  

**In This Role You Will**
* Support Relationship Managers and Senior Bankers in day-to-day client coverage and relationship development across the Nordics
* Produce pitch books, client updates, market overviews, relationship reviews and materials for senior client meetings across financing, markets and advisory products
* Identify marketing opportunities; undertake analysis and help prepare proposals, presentations and pitches for new products and services
* Track client revenues, wallet share, transaction pipelines, financing needs and broader opportunities across the Corporate and Institutional Banking (CIB) platform
* Work closely with Product teams to support origination and delivery of solutions to clients
* Analyse sector trends, market conditions, funding environments, balance sheet developments and key risks impacting institutional clients
* Support credit work including credit applications, profitability calculations and annual credit reviews
* Coordinate internal approvals, KYC/compliance requirements, due diligence requests and transaction workflows; monitor and follow up on pending KYC items
* Maintain CRM systems and internal databases; prepare management reporting, maintain organisational maps and monitor strategic developments relevant to covered clients
* Ensure prompt and appropriate responses to client enquiries by coordinating with product and support teams
* Take an active role in initiatives beyond core responsibilities, proactively seeking stretch assignments to accelerate learning
* Support revenue generation while ensuring activities align with HSBC’s risk appetite and high standards of conduct


**You Should Apply If You Have**
* Initial relevant experience in banking (institutional coverage, relationship management, credit, markets, or a related field)
* Degree in Finance, Economics, Business, Accounting (or similar); CFA or similar qualification is a plus
* Solid understanding of financial markets/capital markets and corporate banking products; ability to analyse financial statements and client performance metrics
* Demonstrated interest in Corporate & Investment Banking and institutional client coverage
* Strong stakeholder management skills and a collaborative, team-first mindset
* Excellent written and verbal communication and presentation skills
* Strong organisation, attention to detail and ability to multi-task in a fast-paced environment
* Advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills (and strong general IT capability)
* Fluency in English; preferred: one Nordic language


 Even if you feel you do not meet 100% of our qualifications, we encourage you to apply, if you believe this role is right for you.
   

  

**What You’ll Get In Return**
 The pay range for this role is SEK 575,000 – SEK 630,000
   

  

 HSBC operates multiple variable pay plans, to reflect the diverse nature of the businesses, roles and markets in which we operate. Variable pay is designed to reward colleagues for their individual performance and contribution, as well as the performance of their relevant Business / Infrastructure, and the overall Group.
   

  

 We offer a comprehensive and competitive package of benefits covering usually covering healthcare, life assurance, pension, and family friendly leaves, as well as many other benefits to support your wellbeing.
   

  

 Being open to different points of view is important for our business and the communities we serve. At HSBC, we’re dedicated to creating diverse and inclusive workplaces. We are committed to removing barriers and ensuring careers at HSBC are inclusive and accessible for everyone to be at their best.
   

  

 If you have a need that requires accommodations or changes during the recruitment process, please let us know.
   

  

 Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.
   

  

 For further information on building your career at HSBC, please visit: www.hsbc.com/careers

title: Data Engineer (Informatica / DB2 / Oracle / GCP)
description:
We are currently looking for an experienced
 **Data Engineer** 
 to join a large-scale data integration team within the
 **financial services sector** 
 . This is an exciting opportunity to work in a complex enterprise environment, contributing to the development and optimization of critical data integration solutions.
 



 📍
 **Location:** 
 Stockholm, Sweden (3 days onsite / 2 days remote)
 



 📅
 **Start Date:** 
 10 August 2026
 



**Language:** 
**English/Full professional & Swedish/Full professional** 




  





**About the Role** 




 As a Data Engineer, you will be responsible for developing, maintaining, and enhancing ETL solutions and data integrations across multiple platforms. You will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, developers, and operations teams to ensure reliable, scalable, and efficient data flows in a highly regulated environment.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Design, develop, and maintain ETL processes and data integration solutions
* Work with large-scale enterprise data platforms and legacy systems
* Ensure data quality, integrity, and performance across integrations
* Collaborate with cross-functional teams to deliver business-critical data solutions
* Support production environments and troubleshoot data-related issues
* Contribute to continuous improvement of data architecture and processes



 Required Skills & Experience
 



 ✔ Strong hands-on experience with
 **Informatica PowerCenter** 




 ✔ Solid SQL development skills
 



 ✔ Experience working with
 **Oracle** 
 and
 **DB2** 
 databases
 



 ✔ Good knowledge of
 **Linux** 
 environments
 



 ✔ Experience with
 **Informatica PowerExchange** 




 ✔ Experience working with
 **Google Cloud Platform (GCP)** 




 ✔ Fluent English communication skills
 



  





**Preferred Qualifications** 




 Experience with regulatory reporting and compliance-related data processes, including:
 


* FATCA
* CRS
* Basel II / Basel III
* Other banking regulatory frameworks



 Experience with
 **Google BigQuery** 




 Experience with:
 


* Tableau
* OPC / Tivoli Workload Scheduler (TWS)
* Mainframe environments
* Production support and operational activities



  





 Why Join?
 


* Long-term assignment within a leading financial services organization
* Opportunity to work on complex enterprise-scale data integration initiatives
* Hybrid work model with flexibility
* Collaborative and highly skilled technical environment



 If you have a strong background in enterprise data engineering and enjoy working with both modern cloud technologies and legacy systems, we'd love to hear from you.

title: Senior Data Engineer
description:
**About Enity Bank Group** 




 At Enity, we believe in people’s potential. We’re challenging the Nordic mortgage market by making banking more inclusive, sustainable, and human. As a listed company with the brands Bluestep Bank, Bank2 and 60plusbanken, we offer mortgages to those often excluded by traditional banks – for example, due to self-employment, employment type, age, or credit history.We operate across Sweden, Norway and Finland, and we’re on a mission to shape the future of banking – together.
 



  





**About The Role** 




 As a
 **Senior Data Engineer** 
 in Enity, you will build and maintain secure and reliable data pipelines and platforms that support business decision-making, reporting, analytics, risk management and compliance. The role involves working with large volumes of financial and other data, ensuring data quality and governance, integrating data from multiple systems, and collaborating with analysts, product teams, and other stakeholders across the bank. In addition, you will help modernize our data architecture, improve performance, and ensure solutions meet regulatory and security requirements.
 



  





 As a data engineer at Enity you will report to the Chief Data Officer and be part of the Data & Analytics team, a tightly knit group of professionals consisting of data engineers, analysts, a product owner, and area leads for Credit, Collection and Valuation.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Design and implement robust data pipelines and data integration solutions.
* Develop and maintain databases and data warehouses.
* Ensure the quality, reliability, and performance of the data infrastructure.
* Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and other stakeholders to understand their data requirements and ensure seamless data flow.
* Implement data governance and security measures to protect sensitive information.



  





**Main tasks** 



* Develop, construct, test, and maintain architectures, such as databases and processing systems.
* Assemble large, complex data sets that meet functional and non-functional business requirements.
* Identify, design, and implement process improvements: automating manual processes, optimizing data delivery, and re-designing infrastructure for greater scalability.



  





**About You** 




 We’re looking for someone who is not only technically strong, but also collaborative, curious, and a hands-on, action-oriented team player who enjoys taking ownership and getting things done.
 



 As a small, tight-knit team, personal characteristics matter a lot — we value team players who take ownership, communicate openly, support their colleagues, and enjoy contributing to a positive and ambitious working environment.
 



  





**Required Skills And Competencies** 



* A minimum of five years of experience working as a data engineer.
* Strong expertise with regards to data pipeline and workflow management tools.
* Knowledge of various data storage and processing solutions (e.g., relational databases, data lakes, etc).
* Strong problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
* Proficiency in SQL, Python, and other programming languages used in data engineering.
* Experience with cloud services such as AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform.
* A collaborative, curious and pragmatic mindset with a strong getting things done mentality.



  





**We Are Really Impressed If You Also** 



* Have experience working with and/or migrating to Microsoft Fabric, or experience with the Microsoft Azure Data stack in general.
* Experience with using and/or enabling LLMs for data engineering and data science tasks, including building and maintaining RAG implementations, agentic systems, etc.
* Proficiency in Swedish is meritorious but not required.



  





**We offer** 




 We offer a competitive compensation package complemented by a generous range of benefits, and some flexibility to support your work-life balance.
 



  





 For this position, we offer a monthly salary starting at SEK 60.000. However, we recognize that experience and unique expertise matter, so there is flexibility to offer a higher salary for the right candidate.
 



  





**Sounds interesting?** 




 We would be happy to see your application as soon as possible as the selection is ongoing. If you would like more information or have questions about the role, please contact HR@enity.com. We look forward to hear from you!
 



*Embracing inclusivity, we value each candidate based on their skills and experience. There is no need to include a photo in your application.*

title: Scrum Master
description:
Job Description
  
Our client is seeking an experienced and proactive Senior Scrum Master / Agile Flow Leader to support a digital product development team focused on delivering data-driven services and user-centric solutions. This opportunity is ideal for professionals who are passionate about agile methodologies, servant leadership, team coaching, and fostering continuous improvement within cross-functional environments.
  
The selected candidate will play a key role in enabling efficient product delivery, enhancing team collaboration, and promoting agile best practices across the organization. You will work closely with Product Owners, Developers, UX Specialists, Analysts, and various business stakeholders to ensure successful delivery of high-quality digital products and services.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Lead and facilitate Agile ceremonies including Sprint Planning, Daily Stand-ups, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives.
  
Guide teams and stakeholders on Scrum principles, Agile frameworks, and continuous improvement practices.
  
Identify delivery challenges and proactively remove impediments impacting team productivity and performance.
  
Collaborate with Product Owners to support backlog refinement, prioritization, and release planning activities.
  
Maintain visibility of project progress, team capacity, delivery commitments, and overall sprint health.
  
Drive initiatives that improve Agile maturity, team effectiveness, and organizational agility.
  
Coordinate cross-team dependencies, risk mitigation activities, and stakeholder alignment efforts.
  
Foster strong collaboration between business units, product management, development teams, and analytics functions.
  
Establish clear processes and structure within complex and rapidly evolving environments.
  
Support effective communication and knowledge sharing among distributed and hybrid teams.
  
  

Required Experience
  
Several years of experience working as a Scrum Master within software development or digital product environments.
  
Proven experience supporting and coaching cross-functional Agile teams.
  
Demonstrated background in Agile transformation, coaching, and change management initiatives.
  
Experience working closely with both technical teams and business stakeholders.
  
Strong understanding of delivery planning, dependency management, and risk management in complex projects.
  
Hands-on experience with Agile collaboration tools such as Jira and Confluence.
  
  

Education
  
Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, Business, or a related field is preferred.
  
Equivalent professional experience will also be considered.
  
  

Required Skills
  
Certified Scrum Master (CSM) or equivalent Scrum certification.
  
Strong knowledge of Scrum framework and Agile methodologies.
  
Agile Coaching and Team Facilitation.
  
Stakeholder Management.
  
Change Management.
  
Risk and Dependency Management.
  
Sprint Planning and Backlog Management.
  
Jira and Confluence.
  
Product Delivery Management.
  
Cross-functional Team Leadership.
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
Experience working with data-driven product teams.
  
Exposure to digital services, analytics platforms, or user-focused product development.
  
Experience within scaled Agile environments.
  
Knowledge of Product Discovery, Product Development, or User Experience (UX) practices.
  
Experience collaborating with distributed or hybrid teams.
  
  

Language Requirements
  
English – Professional/Fluent Level (Mandatory)
  
  

Personal Attributes
  
  

**The ideal candidate should possess:**  

Strong servant leadership qualities.
  
Excellent coaching and mentoring capabilities.
  
Structured and solution-oriented mindset.
  
Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
  
Ability to build relationships across multiple stakeholder groups.
  
Adaptability and pragmatism when navigating changing priorities.
  
Confidence in constructively challenging existing ways of working.
  
Ability to inspire engagement, teamwork, and continuous progress.
  
  

Application Requirements
  
  

**Interested candidates should submit:**  

Updated CV in English.
  
Completed Candidate Template in Word Format.
  
  

**Job Mode:** Hybrid (Approximately 50% On-site and 50% Remote)
  
**Application Method:** Interested candidates can apply by sending their profile to hr@semiconservicenordic.com

title: Erfaren UX-designer till Metria
description:
Vill du designa tjänster som hjälper människor att fatta bättre beslut om mark, natur och miljö?


På Metria arbetar vi med geodata, GIS och digitala tjänster där kartor, platser och komplex information står i centrum. Våra lösningar används bland annat inom samhällsbyggnad, riskanalys och planering. Nu söker vi en erfaren UX-designer till ett team som arbetar med digitala tjänster inom naturskydd.


Hos oss får du arbeta nära kollegor, beställare och användare. Vi har god tillgång till referensgrupper och slutanvändare, vilket gör att vi kan förstå behov, testa idéer tidigt och bygga lösningar som fungerar i vardagen.

**Om rollen**


Som UX-designer hos oss är du med från tidig behovsförståelse till färdig lösning. Du jobbar nära verksamhetsexperter, tech lead, utvecklare, projektledare/product managers, kravanalytiker, beställare och andra designers.


Teamet arbetar med tjänster där geografisk information, komplexa verksamhetsflöden, roller, behörigheter och kartbaserade vyer behöver hänga ihop på ett begripligt sätt.


Du kommer bland annat att:

* kartlägga användarresor och analysera behov
* genomföra intervjuer, användningstester och discovery-arbete
* skapa prototyper och testa idéer
* arbeta med GIS-nära flöden, informationsmodeller och rollberoenden
* hjälpa teamet att omvandla insikter till konkreta backlog items

Vi arbetar framför allt i Figma, Figma Make, Claude Design och Miro. Vi prototypar också allt mer med stöd av AI, så vi tror att du redan utforskar hur AI kan göra designarbetet snabbare och bättre.

**Vem är du?**


Du är en trygg och erfaren designer som kan driva ditt arbete framåt. Du är analytisk, nyfiken och bra på att skapa struktur när saker är komplexa eller otydliga.


Du tycker om att samarbeta och ser design som något vi gör tillsammans. Du kan argumentera för en lösning när det behövs, men är också prestigelös, lyhörd och pragmatisk.


Du har också en bred förståelse för hur digitala produkter byggs. Du behöver inte vara utvecklare, men du har grundläggande koll på hur information rör sig genom ett system.


Rätt personlighet och inställning är viktigare än att du checkar av alla punkter, men vi ser gärna att du har:

* vana av intervjuer, användningstester och discovery-arbete
* erfarenhet av komplexa system
* god förståelse för informationsarkitektur och informationsmodeller
* erfarenhet av GIS, geografisk information eller kartbaserade tjänster
* vana av Figma, prototyping och AI inom designarbetet
* förmåga att uttrycka dig tydligt på svenska i tal och skrift

Erfarenhet av kravanalys, projektledning, mätning och uppföljning av användning är meriterande.

**Vilka är vi?**


På Metria skapar vi digitala tjänster som hjälper våra kunder att fatta bättre beslut med stöd av geografisk information och fastighetsdata. För oss är användarupplevelsen en central del av produktutvecklingen, och vi strävar efter att göra komplex information enkel att förstå och använda.


Hos oss arbetar du nära både användare, verksamhet och utvecklingsteam. Det innebär korta beslutsvägar, stort inflytande och möjlighet att påverka riktningen för våra produkter. Vi tror på tvärfunktionellt samarbete, kunskapsutbyte och ett arbetsliv där flexibilitet och gemenskap kan gå hand i hand.

**Om tjänsten**


Placeringsort är Luleå. För rätt person finns eventuellt möjlighet till placering i Umeå eller Stockholm.


Tjänsten är på heltid och tills vidare, med sex månaders provanställning.


Vi går igenom ansökningar löpande, så vänta inte med att höra av dig.


Har du frågor om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Jenny Odeblom, Head of UX, på jenny.odeblom@metria.se.


Välkommen till Metria!

title: Senior UX & Interaction Designer
description:
**About Justera Group**  

Justera Group AB is a leading Swedish IT consulting firm with great experience specializing in building high-performing technology teams and delivering end-to-end IT solutions. Led by dynamic female executives, Justera Group focuses on helping businesses harness the power of digital technologies to continuously evolve in today’s rapidly changing economy. The company has deep expertise in IT consultancy, software development, and recruitment services, providing clients with purpose-driven, well-trained consultants who consistently deliver efficient and effective results. Justera Group manages the entire talent lifecycle, from hiring to ongoing management, ensuring the right fit for each project on time and within budget. With their strong commitment to quality, collaboration, and client success, Justera Group is a trusted partner for technology innovation across Sweden.  

  

**About the Role:**


We are seeking an experienced Senior UX & Interaction Designer to join a forward-thinking Design and Digital Experience team focused on shaping the future of mobility. This is an opportunity to work within a highly collaborative, cross-functional environment where creativity, innovation, and user-centered design drive the development of next-generation digital experiences.


The successful candidate will contribute to both production-ready user interface solutions and visionary concepts that define future digital experience platforms. You will play a key role in creating seamless interactions across digital and physical touchpoints, including infotainment systems, driver information interfaces, connected services, and emerging mobility experiences.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* Lead the design and evolution of Human-Machine Interface (HMI) solutions across digital products and platforms.
* Develop, evaluate, and document innovative multi-modal interaction concepts and information architectures.
* Assess existing HMI frameworks, identify improvement opportunities, and support implementation activities.
* Define and execute user research initiatives, selecting appropriate methodologies based on project goals.
* Translate user research findings into actionable insights that drive iterative design improvements.
* Create detailed interaction models, user flows, wireframes, prototypes, and design specifications.
* Contribute to and maintain scalable design systems and implementation guidelines.
* Collaborate closely with designers, researchers, engineers, product managers, and business stakeholders.
* Apply systems-thinking principles to design cohesive user experiences across complex customer journeys.
* Lead design activities and provide guidance to other designers within the team.
* Drive projects independently while balancing multiple priorities and deadlines.

**Requirements:**

* Extensive experience in UX and Interaction Design, preferably within automotive, mobility, or other complex digital ecosystems.
* Strong understanding of usability principles and user-centered design methodologies.
* Proven experience planning, conducting, and synthesizing UX research and user testing.
* Solid knowledge of automotive HMI design processes, standards, and best practices.
* Experience working with design systems and creating implementation-ready specifications.
* Demonstrated ability to lead design initiatives and mentor team members.
* Strong project planning and stakeholder management capabilities.
* Portfolio showcasing digital product design, interaction frameworks, and complex UX problem-solving.
* Experience collaborating within multidisciplinary product development teams.

**What We Offer:**

* A dynamic and innovative workplace driving advancements in cutting-edge robotic technologies
* The opportunity to collaborate with talented cross-functional teams on meaningful and impactful projects
* Competitive compensation and comprehensive benefits package
* A supportive culture that fosters continuous learning, growth, and professional development
* Flexible hybrid working model for an optimal work-life balance
* 25 days of annual leave to recharge and relax
* An annual wellness allowance of 3,500 SEK to support your health and wellbeing

title: Web3 Product Manager
description:
Bitrefill is remote-first. This is full-time. You'll need solid overlap with both European and Latin American time zones.

### **What is Bitrefill?**


Bitrefill lets people spend crypto on real stuff: gift cards, mobile top-ups, subscriptions, bill payments. 180+ countries. Profitable since launch. Over 12 years in and still building.


The team is about 90 people, fully distributed. Product decisions are made close to the work. The role sits within a focused squad of 5-7 people: engineers and designers who take pride in what they ship. Few meetings. Lots of autonomy.

**The job**
-----------


Bitrefill's early product decisions came from people who live on crypto themselves. That intuition built the foundation, but the company has reached a scale where intuition alone is not enough. This role is about turning scattered knowledge of crypto-native users into a systematic product strategy.


The market is also shifting. DeFi users are moving from speculation toward investing. Yield positions, airdrop proceeds, stablecoin salaries: real wealth that people want to use without friction. Bitrefill needs a product manager who understands this transition and can build for it.  

  

**THE HARD PROBLEMS**  

  

This role exists because these challenges, and many more, remain open:

* Users hold assets across Arbitrum, Base, Solana, and elsewhere. Checkout needs to feel simple without obscuring the complexity that actually matters.
* WalletConnect sessions drop, signing flows vary across wallets, and mobile browser behavior is inconsistent. A payment experience that works reliably across these conditions does not yet exist.
* When someone claims an airdrop or exits a lending position, they have liquidity. Reaching them at the right moment, without being intrusive, is an unsolved design problem.
* High-value users transact thousands per month. A $2,000 gift card purchase requires confidence that the system will work. That trust must be built into the product itself.

**What you'll do**
------------------

* Own product discovery, planning, and delivery for crypto-native features
* Conduct direct research with users who spend crypto daily: understand their setups, workarounds, and frustrations
* Write specifications with clear requirements and acceptance criteria; manage the backlog; ship end-to-end alongside engineering and design
* Make integration decisions about chains, wallets, and protocols, based on usage data rather than market hype
* Facilitate cross-functional coordination with compliance, marketing, support, and operations
* Represent Bitrefill in crypto communities and at industry conferences

**Who you are**
---------------

**Experience**

* 8+ years in product management, ideally in payments, e-commerce, or fintech
* Track record of shipping products with real adoption
* Experience leading work across distributed teams and time zones
* Practical experience with DeFi: vaults, collateralized positions, bridging, gas dynamics
* Familiarity with wallet UX across desktop and mobile
* Ability to decompose large problems into shippable increments
* Skill in facilitating alignment across engineering, design, and business functions
* Clear written communication: specifications, decision documents, async updates

**Working with AI**

* AI is already part of the workflow: drafting specifications, building quick prototypes, improving written communication
* Able to close the gap between product thinking and technical implementation
* Self-directed use, not waiting for an AI strategy to be handed down

**Mindset**

* Skepticism toward hype, curiosity toward actual usage patterns
* Preference for shipping small things that work over large things that do not
* Comfort with ambiguity, paired with the ability to create clarity for others

**Why Bitrefill?**
------------------


We've been making crypto actually useful since 2014. Remote-first before it was cool. You get real ownership, real trust, and a team that ships.


The work is challenging. The people are sharp and creative. There's always someone to bounce ideas off, plus regular gatherings, meetups, and conferences to stay connected beyond the screen.


If you're wondering whether this is for you:

* Who we are
* How we work
* Our hiring process

title: Creative Designer
description:
**About Air Apps**
 At Air Apps, we believe in thinking bigger—and moving faster. We’re a family-founded company on a mission to create the world’s first AI-powered Personal & Entrepreneurial Resource Planner (PRP), and we need your passion and ambition to help us change how people plan, work, and live. Born in Lisbon, Portugal in 2018—and now with offices in both Lisbon and San Francisco—we’ve remained self-funded while reaching over 100 million downloads worldwide.
   

  

 Our long-term focus drives us to challenge the status quo every day, pushing the boundaries of AI-driven solutions that truly make a difference. Here, you’ll be a creative force, shaping products that empower people across the globe.
   

  

 Join us on this journey to redefine resource management—and change lives along the way.
   

  

**The Role**
 As a Creative Designer at Air Apps, you will play a crucial role in shaping our brand identity and delivering visually engaging designs across multiple platforms. You will collaborate with various teams to ensure a cohesive and compelling user experience, working on digital ads, social media content, landing pages, email campaigns, and print materials.
   

  

* This is a fully onsite position, based at our office in Lisbon, where you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams in person and contribute to a dynamic and fast-paced environment. We are open to support with relocation efforts.


**Responsibilities**
* Develop and maintain a consistent visual identity that aligns with Air Apps’ brand guidelines.
* Create engaging graphics, illustrations, infographics, and layouts for web, social media, email campaigns, and print materials.
* Collaborate with Marketing, Product, and Content teams to translate requirements into compelling visual solutions.
* Iterate on designs based on feedback from stakeholders and refine assets using user insights and performance metrics.
* Ensure high-quality output and conduct design quality assurance across various channels.
* Utilize industry-standard design software (e.g., Adobe Creative Suite, Figma) to create and deliver assets in multiple formats.


**Requirements**
* Bachelor's degree in Graphic Design, Fine Arts, or a related field.
* Around 3 years of experience creating assets for digital marketing campaigns, social media, and web.
* Strong portfolio showcasing a range of design projects and styles.
* Knowledge of typography, color theory, layout, and design best practices.
* Proficiency in industry-standard design tools such as Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and Figma.
* Strong communication, time-management, and attention-to-detail skills.
* Ability to work in a fast-paced, remote environment and collaborate effectively across teams.


**What benefits are we offering?**
* Apple hardware ecosystem for work.
* Annual Bonus
* Top-tier Health and Life Insurance for peace of mind.
* Transportation Budget to support your commute needs.
* Coverflex benefits package for meal allowances, well-being, and more.
* Childcare support.
* Air Conference - an opportunity to meet the team, collaborate, and grow together.
* Pension Fund to support your long-term financial planning.
* Urban Sports Club membership to keep you active.
* Meals 100% free at the hub.


**Diversity & Inclusion**
 At Air Apps, we are committed to fostering a diverse, inclusive, and equitable workplace. We enthusiastically welcome applicants from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We celebrate diversity in all its forms and believe that varied voices and experiences make us stronger.
   

  

**Application Disclaimer**
 At Air Apps, we value transparency and integrity in our hiring process. Applicants must submit their own work without any AI-generated assistance. Any use of AI in application materials, assessments, or interviews will result in disqualification.
   

  

 Compensation Range: €44K - €55K

title: Product Manager
description:
At Billogram, we believe it always pays off to treat people well. That’s why we’ve built a payment platform that covers the full lifecycle, from the first payment to full resolution. We make money when our clients’ customers pay on time, not from the friction when they don’t. Whether it’s recurring payments, consumer invoicing, or enterprise billing, the principle is the same: one platform, designed to keep payments working and relationships growing.
   

  

 As we continue to evolve our product organization, we are looking for a Product Manager to join our team in Stockholm.
   

  

**About the role:**
 As a Product Manager at Billogram, your mission is to deliver real value for both our customers and our business. You will lead the development of the core parts of the recipient experience, including invoice distribution, email communication, web invoice design, and smart dunning flows.
   

  

 In this role, you own the most critical touchpoints where our product meets the end-user. Success here requires more than just shipping features; it’s about using data and customer insights to ensure that every part of the journey is optimized for conversion and a great user experience. Collaborating closely with engineers in your team, other product teams, and departments, you’ll align priorities and drive cross-functional success to move the needle on our most important metrics.
   

  

**Primary responsibilities:**
* Optimize the End-User Experience: Drive the development of our invoice distribution, email flows, and web invoice design to ensure maximum conversion and user satisfaction.
* Champion Data-Driven Discovery: Implement tracking and lead A/B testing to move from assumptions to validated insights.
* Strategic & Operational Balance: Lead your team through product discovery while also being hands-on in managing incidents and operational excellence.
* Business Impact: Understand how your part of the product contributes to Billogram's revenue and use that business acumen to prioritize the most valuable initiatives.
* AI Integration: Use AI in your daily work to automate research and prototyping, while continuously refining your expertise to ensure Billogram remains at the forefront of AI-driven product management.


**What we are looking for:**
 You value progress over perfection, understanding that great products are built through continuous learning and smart iterations. You have a natural urge to get to the bottom of things and use data to navigate through the noise. For you, success is defined by actual impact on the business and our users, rather than the volume of features delivered.
   

  

 Navigating ambiguity comes naturally to you, and you take full ownership of your area, staying proactive even when responsibility is unclear. As a skilled communicator, you find it easy to align people toward a common goal, whether discussing technical logic or commercial value. You are also curious about how the PM role is evolving and enjoy exploring new tools, such as AI, to enhance your way of working.
   

  

**Skills & traits which will help you succeed in this role:**
* Product Management Expertise: Minimum of 3 years of documented experience in leading both discovery and delivery processes with a focus on creating measurable value.
* Payment Experience: Prior experience with payment solutions such as card payments, Open Banking, digital billing, dunning flows, or debt collection is a strong advantage.
* Cross-functional Collaboration: A proven ability to build bridges between engineering, design, and commercial teams to foster alignment and deliver high-value outcomes.
* Data-driven Mindset: Experience in using customer insights and metrics to guide decisions and track product success.
* Communication: Fluency in written and spoken English is required. Fluency in Swedish is a plus.
* Relevant Background: Experience from FinTech, regulated businesses, or a fast-growing start-up or scale-up environment is highly beneficial.


**Why work with us?**
 At Billogram we promise you an exciting journey as we break into new markets and grow at a rapid pace. That aside you will get lots of freedom and opportunity to develop together with great colleagues at our office, right in the center of Stockholm. We also offer flexible working hours, exciting challenges and a whole lot of laughs.
   

  

 We're committed to your long-term development, offering clear career progression paths. You'll also enjoy a fantastic benefits package, including 30 (+2) days of holiday, an occupational pension, a generous wellness allowance, sick leave top-up, and parental leave compensation. At Billogram, our inclusive and diverse culture ensures that everyone can enjoy a fulfilling career and a great work-life balance.
   

  

*At Billogram, we provide equal opportunities and strive for an inclusive and diverse environment. All applicants will be considered for employment without attention to gender, transgender identity or expression, ethnicity, religion or other beliefs, disability, sexual orientation, or age.*
 Welcome to the world of Billogram!

title: Product Manager, Courier Delivery Experience
description:
**About Wolt**
 At Wolt, we create technology that brings joy, simplicity and earnings to the neighborhoods of the world. In 2014 we started with delivery of restaurant food. Now we’re building the delivery of (almost) everything and you’ll find us in over 500 cities in 30 countries around the world. In 2022 we joined forces with DoorDash and together we keep on dreaming big and expanding across the globe.
   

  

 Working at Wolt isn’t always easy, but it’s definitely exciting. Here you’ll learn more, build more, and ship more than in most other companies. You’ll be challenged a lot, but also have a lot of fun on the way. So, if you’re a self-starter with drive and entrepreneurial spirit, this could be the ride of your life.
   

  

 We are looking for a Product Manager to join our Courier and Delivery Experience team. This role will focus on ensuring world-class courier and consumer delivery experiences while optimizing the delivery process for operational excellence. The team’s focus is on enhancing key touchpoints in the courier journey, ensuring seamless delivery experiences, and developing product innovations that improve Courier and Consumer experiences and reliability.
   

  

 You will play a crucial role in shaping the courier experience, ensuring that our couriers feel valued and supported while enhancing the efficiency of deliveries. By blending operational expertise with a deep understanding of courier needs, you will improve the user journey from onboarding couriers to completing deliveries. From designing new features that boost courier satisfaction to refining delivery processes, your work will contribute to key metrics across courier engagement, performance, and satisfaction.
   

  

 The ideal candidate will have an exceptional track record in product management, with proven success in working with cross-functional teams across engineering, design, product, and operations. You will define the vision and roadmap for courier and delivery experiences across multiple projects. Working in partnership with business stakeholders, you will also collaborate on cross-team initiatives, contributing to the broader business strategy while ensuring a deep understanding of the courier experience.
   

  

**Our humble expectations**
 You could come to this role from various backgrounds, but ideally, you bring:
   

  

* Several years of experience in product management, specializing in consumer-facing products, courier services, delivery systems, or logistics.
* Demonstrated expertise in supporting cross-functional teams and fostering operational excellence in fast-paced, high-demand industries.
* Proven track record in building products influencing cross-functional teams, including engineering, data, design, and operations.
* Expertise in developing user-centric products, with the ability to address complex user experience-related challenges.
* High ownership and collaborative capabilities, making thoughtful decisions, and focusing on product details to ensure execution aligns with the vision.
* Strong ability to define, execute, and measure meaningful OKRs, ensuring alignment with company-wide goals and fostering bottom-up alignment from your team.
* Ability to focus on the bigger picture, creating solutions that benefit couriers, operations, and customer experiences across the organization.
* Excellent stakeholder management skills, able to work effectively with diverse internal teams and external partners, while confidently engaging with leadership in a high-visibility role.


 The position will be filled as soon as we find the right person, so make sure to apply as soon as you realize you really, really want to join us!
   

  

 The compensation will be a negotiable combination of monthly pay and DoorDash RSUs. The latter makes it exceptionally easy to be excited about our company growing and doing well, as you’ll own a piece of the pie.
   

  

 Our Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion
   

  

 We’re committed to growing and empowering a more inclusive community within our company, industry, and cities. That’s why we hire and cultivate diverse teams of people from all backgrounds, experiences, and perspectives. We believe that true innovation happens when everyone has room at the table and the tools, resources, and opportunity to excel.

title: Senior Product Manager – Infrastructure
description:
**Epassi’s purpose is to boost everyday well-being** 
 .
   

  

 We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions in Europe. We were established in Finland in 2007, and in 2008 we were the first company to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in Europe. Since then, we have consistently grown, diversifying our products and introducing our services into new markets. Epassi has been awarded by the Financial Times as one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe on multiple occasions.
   

  

**Are you interested in joining Europe’s fastest growing companies? Do you want to impact the wellbeing of millions of employees?**
 If yes, then keep reading and join us to make an impact in a fast-paced and dynamic environment where you’ll play a key role in boosting everyday wellbeing, at home, at work. Everywhere!
   

  

**Epassi’s** 
 purpose is to boost everyday well-being. We are a leading provider of employee benefit solutions across Europe. We were established in Finland in 2007, and in 2008 we were the first company to launch a mobile-payable employee benefit payment solution in Europe. Since then, we have consistently grown, diversifying our products and introducing our services into new markets.
   

  

 Epassi has been awarded by the Financial Times as one of the fastest-growing companies in Europe on multiple occasions.
   

  

**We are currently hiring a Senior Product Manager – Infrastructure to be based in any of Epassi's European locations**
**Your mission at Epassi**
 As a Senior Product Manager for Infrastructure, you will own the roadmap for the internal and external developer experience at Epassi — ensuring that engineering teams across all Epassi markets can build, deploy, and debug with speed and confidence. You’ll work as part of our platform product team alongside other product managers, collaborating closely with engineering and infrastructure to deliver the tooling, APIs, and platform services that power how Epassi’s products are built and operated.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Will Be**
* Run discovery with dev teams across all Epassi countries to identify real deployment and debugging friction.
* Own the platform infrastructure backlog — what gets built, for which teams, in what order — with clear, documented rationale.
* Define developer experience metrics: deployment frequency, time-to-debug, and service onboarding time — not just uptime.
* Own product decisions for the notifications service, email templates, API contracts, and feature flag service.
* Write business cases for platform investments and support the Head of Product on strategy.
* Understand infrastructure concepts — latency, funnels, service architecture — well enough to identify where they create developer friction and translate that into actionable product decisions.


**You’ll Be Successful In This Role If You**
* Drive adoption over delivery — platform tooling is actively used by the teams it was built for. Shipped features without adoption are failures.
* Move DX metrics — measurable improvement in deployment frequency, time-to-debug, and onboarding time across pilot teams.
* Build a trusted backlog — teams across all Epassi countries understand prioritisation decisions and rarely push back as arbitrary.
* Expand rollout on schedule — phased expansion from pilot services proceeds without major adoption failures or rollbacks.
* Make ownership unambiguous — each platform surface has a clear decision log, versioning policy, and no confusion about who decides what.


**What You’ll Be Doing (daily)**
* Lead and guide product development teams to deliver high-value features and platform elements.
* Define and refine business and technical requirements with stakeholders, balancing value, cost, and quality.
* Build and maintain a well-prioritised product backlog and collaborate with teams to create executable release plans.
* Facilitate sprint planning, reviews, and retrospectives in an agile/Scrum environment.
* Share insights and best practices to continuously improve innovation processes.


**What You Should Bring**
* Relevant technical degree (B.Sc. or M.Sc.), e.g. Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related field.
* 5+ years of experience in product management, platform development, or a related technical discipline.
* Experience leading teams or projects in agile/Scrum environments.
* Proven track record in product management, ideally in a platform or infrastructure context.
* Experience defining and refining business and technical requirements with cross-functional stakeholders.
* Background in managing product backlogs and building executable release plans.
* Experience presenting to senior leaders of a company.


**Your Track Record**
* Proven track record in platform or infrastructure product management, with clear examples of improving developer experience at scale.
* Experience managing end-to-end product cycles from discovery through adoption.
* Background in driving measurable outcomes through data-driven prioritisation.
* Familiarity with agile/Scrum methodologies and process frameworks.
* Knowledge of AI tools such as Claude and how they can enhance product management workflows and decision-making.


**Your Competency Profile**
* Customer-focused mindset: passionate about building secure, scalable products for both internal and external users.
* Analytical rigour: structured, data-driven approach to problem-solving with strong attention to outcomes.
* Communication & collaboration: able to align diverse stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams.
* Entrepreneurial spirit: proactive, self-driven, and comfortable with ambiguity in a fast-growing environment.
* Growth mindset: continuously learning, sharing knowledge, and driving improvement across teams.
* Adaptability: willingness to travel as required across European locations.


**Why You Should Join Us**
 Be part of an ambitious growth journey and join a global community of driven, bold, entrepreneurial, yet humble Epassians in a company that is constantly evolving.
   

  

* Work with a clear purpose — boosting everyday wellbeing — and create a meaningful, positive impact on both individuals and society.
* Thrive in an open and inclusive culture with low hierarchy, where relationships are built on respect, camaraderie, and shared successes and challenges.
* Come as you are — we’ll bring out the best in you and support your personal and professional growth.
* Join a fast-growing tech company with a strong financial foundation, offering opportunities to grow and make an impact together with us.
* Benefit from an inspiring and supportive work environment where employee wellbeing is a true priority.
* Contribute to solidifying our position as the number one player in Europe’s employee benefit market and help shape the next wave of success.

title: Verification and Validation Manager
description:
Do you have experience in software test management and want to improve cancer care for patients around the world? Join RaySearch's development department as Verification and Validation Manager for RayCare!
   

  

**About The Position**
 RayCare is an innovative oncology information system designed to support clinical workflows at cancer clinics. It is developed by multiple agile teams working closely together, with expertise spanning clinical workflows, integrations, and system architecture. We develop our products in close collaboration with leading cancer clinics worldwide.
   

  

 As Verification and Validation Manager for RayCare, you will be part of the RayCare Project Management team and play a central role in ensuring product quality throughout the development lifecycle. Together with the team, you will plan, lead, and document development projects, ensuring that we deliver safe and high-quality software to our users.
   

  

 In this role, you will work closely with development teams, product management, and other stakeholders across the organization. The work is dynamic and cross-functional, requiring both structure and adaptability.
   

  

**Your main tasks**
* Own and evolve the test strategy for RayCare development projects, including increased automation.
* Lead verification and validation activities for new RayCare releases, ensuring compliance with development and risk management processes.
* Ensure stable, fit-for-purpose test and stage environments, including reliable integration behavior across deployments. Coordinate and support test execution across teams, including usability testing and V&V documentation (e.g. test specifications, reports, and V&V summaries


**Your profile**
 We believe that you have a background as a test manager or a similar leading role within software testing. You are pragmatic in your approach and able to structure and prioritize work in a dynamic environment.
   

  

 You enjoy solving problems as they arise and take initiative to improve ways of working— - especially when it comes to automation and efficiency. You are comfortable coordinating across with teams and stakeholders and have strong communication skills.
   

  

**Requirements**
* BSc or MSc degree in engineering, or equivalent
* 5+ years of experience in test management within software development focusing on test automation
* Excellent written and spoken communication skills (English and Swedish)


**Meriting**
* Strong experience driving test automation and working with system integrations in complex environments
* Experience coordinating usability testing and working within agile development methodologies
* Background in the MedTech industry with hands-on experience using Azure DevOps Server


**Our Culture**
 Culture at RaySeach is the driving force behind our organization, where everything we do is driven by a shared passion for innovation and the fight against cancer. Our dedication is reflected in our ability to deliver exceptional results, pay close attention to detail, and consistently go the extra mile. Our employees stand out as experts in their field, driven by a relentless focus on solving problems - no matter how complex. At RaySearch, we take pride in leading the way in cancer treatment, leveraging cutting-edge technology to develop innovative solutions that make a real difference in patient care.
   

  

**Our Offer**
 At RaySearch, we offer a diverse and inclusive work environment, fostering openness, sincerity, and collaboration. Located in Hagastaden, Stockholm's Life Science Hub, our modern and creative workspace includes an in-house gym, yoga, and social activities like ping pong, table football, and regular after-work events. Our bistro serves a fantastic lunch buffet, and we offer morning- and afternoon-fika every day. Our rooftop terrace also provides a stunning 360-degree view of Stockholm, enhancing the work experience. All of this comes attached with a competitive compensation and benefits package.
   

  

**Application**
 Please apply to the position through the application form below. Selection and interviews will be ongoing. We do not accept applications by email.

title: Experienced Data Engineer (Informatica PowerCenter)
description:
Job Description
  
Our client is seeking an experienced Data Engineer to join a large-scale data integration team within the financial services sector. This role is ideal for a professional with a strong background in enterprise data environments and expertise in ETL development, database technologies, and system integrations.
  
The successful candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, maintaining, and optimizing data integration solutions across multiple platforms and business systems. You will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, development teams, and operational support functions to ensure efficient, reliable, and scalable data processing capabilities within a complex technical landscape.
  
The environment includes legacy and modern technologies such as DB2, Mainframe, Linux, Oracle, Informatica PowerCenter, and cloud-based solutions.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Develop, enhance, and maintain ETL workflows and enterprise data integration solutions.
  
Design and optimize data processing pipelines to support business and regulatory requirements.
  
Collaborate with business users and technical teams to gather requirements and translate them into effective data solutions.
  
Ensure data quality, consistency, and reliability across multiple applications and platforms.
  
Perform SQL development, query optimization, and database-related activities within large-scale environments.
  
Support integration activities involving Oracle, DB2, Mainframe, Linux, and cloud-based systems.
  
Troubleshoot and resolve data processing issues while ensuring minimal disruption to business operations.
  
Participate in system enhancements, upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives.
  
Work closely with operations and support teams to maintain stable production environments.
  
Contribute to technical documentation, knowledge sharing, and best-practice implementation within the team.
  
  

Required Experience
  
Extensive experience as a Data Engineer or ETL Developer in enterprise-scale environments.
  
Strong hands-on experience with Informatica PowerCenter.
  
Proven expertise in SQL development and database programming.
  
Experience working with Oracle and DB2 databases.
  
Good working knowledge of Linux operating systems.
  
Hands-on experience with Informatica PowerExchange.
  
Experience working with Google Cloud Platform (GCP).
  
Previous exposure to complex legacy environments, including Mainframe-based systems.
  
  

Education
  
Bachelor's Degree or Master's Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Software Engineering, Data Engineering, or a related discipline.
  
Equivalent professional experience may also be considered.
  
  

Required Skills
  
Informatica PowerCenter
  
Informatica PowerExchange
  
SQL Development
  
ETL Development & Data Integration
  
Oracle Database
  
DB2 Database
  
Linux Administration and Scripting
  
Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
  
Business Intelligence
  
Data Warehousing
  
Database Performance Optimization
  
Data Analysis and Troubleshooting
  
  

Preferred Qualifications
  
The following experience will be considered highly advantageous:
  
Experience with banking regulatory reporting and compliance frameworks such as FATCA, CRS, Basel II, Basel III, or similar financial regulations.
  
Strong knowledge of Google Cloud Platform services, particularly BigQuery.
  
Experience with Tableau reporting and analytics solutions.
  
Exposure to OPC or Tivoli Workload Scheduler (TWS).
  
Previous experience working within Mainframe environments.
  
Involvement in production support, operational maintenance, or incident management activities.
  
  

Language Requirements
  
English – Fluent (Mandatory)
  
Swedish – Professional Working Proficiency (Preferred/Required for local collaboration)
  
  

Personal Attributes
  
  

**The ideal candidate should demonstrate:**  

Strong self-motivation and accountability.
  
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
  
Ability to work effectively within cross-functional teams.
  
Analytical mindset with strong problem-solving capabilities.
  
Attention to detail and commitment to data quality.
  
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a complex enterprise environment.
  
Collaborative approach with a focus on continuous improvement.
  
  

Additional Information
  
* Job Mode: Hybrid (75% On-site / 25% Remote)


**Work Schedule:** Full-Time (40 Hours per Week)
  
  

**Application Method:** Interested candidates can apply by sending their profile to hr@semiconservicenordic.com

title: Erfaren PLC-programmerare (Egenkonsult / B2B) – Smålandsstenar
description:
Vi söker nu en PLC-programmerare med starkt fokus på utveckling till vår kund (en etablerad och stabil industrikoncern i Smålandsstenar som utvecklar automatiserade lagersystem och mekanisk hanterings utrustning.)
  
  

Företaget har idag en intern PLC-funktion på plats som sköter den dagliga driften, men behöver nu utöka teamet för att bygga bort sårbarhet och accelerera sina utvecklingsprojekt. Rollerna kommer att delas upp tydligt: den interna programmeraren fokuserar på produktion och underhåll, medan ditt uppdrag blir en renodlad utvecklingsroll där du fokuserar på att vidareutveckla systemarkitekturen och industrialisera nya automationskoncept.
  
  

**Viktigt för detta uppdrag:** Verksamheten är en fysisk tillverkande industri och teamet är i dagsläget inte rustat för distansarbete. Uppdraget kräver därför att du bor på pendlingsavstånd och kan arbeta helt på plats i Smålandsstenar.
  
  

️ Dina arbetsuppgifter
  
**Utveckling & Nykonstruktion:** Ansvara för programmering och vidareutveckling av PLC-system för maskiner, transportband, skannersystem och integrerad lagerutrustning.
  
  

**Arkitektur & Systemdesign:** Fungera som ett tekniskt bollplank vid val av framtida automationslösningar tillsammans med företagets tekniska ledning.
  
  

**Redundans:** Dokumentera och strukturera kod för att säkra och tillgängliggöra företagets automationskapacitet framåt.
  
  

Obs! Den dagliga felavhjälpningen och det löpande underhållet i fabriken hanteras primärt av den interna teknikern.
  
  

Vem är du?
  
Vi söker dig som är en trygg egenkonsult med gedigen erfarenhet av maskinnära programmering och industriell automation.
  
  

**Erfarenhet:** Dokumenterad erfarenhet av PLC-programmering med fokus på utveckling och nykonstruktion (gärna inom tillverkande industri, logistiksystem eller transportsystem).
  
  

**Teknisk kompetens:** Stabil grund inom PLC-programmering samt god förståelse för hur mjukvara och hårdvara (transporter, motorer, sensorer) samverkar.
  
  

**Mentalitet:** Du är självgående, pragmatisk och trivs bäst när du får arbeta hands-on nära den fysiska utrustningen.
  
  

**Språk:** Svenska eller engelska i tal och skrift.
  
  

Varför detta uppdrag via Teampilot?
  
Här kliver du in som nyckelperson i ett mycket välskött bolag med korta beslutsvägar och starka, finansiella ägare i ryggen. Du får fokusera helt på nyutveckling i en miljö där dina insatser gör direkt skillnad för produktionen.
  
  

**Omfattning:** Heltid
  
**Uppdragstyp:** B2B-kontrakt (Fakturering via eget bolag)
  
**Start:** Enligt överenskommelse
  
**Plats:** 100% På plats i Smålandsstenar

title: GRC Director & CISO till Visma Publitech
description:
*Vill du kombinera ett strategiskt säkerhetsansvar med att göra verklig samhällsnytta? På Publitech utvecklar vi system som håller igång Sveriges kommuner och regioner. Du kliver in i en spännande fas där vi transformerar vår verksamhet mot att bli AI-native. I rollen som GRC Director & CISO tar du ett helhetsgrepp om våra regelverk och cyberrisker, samtidigt som du leder ett engagerat team och fortsätter att bygga en säkerhetskultur där compliance fungerar som en direkt affärsmöjliggörare.*

### **Om rollen**


Vi går just nu in i en spännande utvecklingsfas där vi rustar Publitech för framtiden. För att stärka vårt arbete ytterligare inom legal, säkerhet och compliance etablerar vi nu denna nya nyckelroll. Som GRC Director & CISO har du det övergripande ansvaret för att Publitech agerar med hög affärsmässig integritet och säkerhet. Du stöttar inte bara affären, utan säkerställer proaktivt att vi gör affärer som är långsiktigt hållbara, säkra och i linje med både lagkrav och vår riskaptit.


Din främsta uppgift är att omsätta externa lagkrav, standarder och koncernens riktlinjer till säker mjukvaruutveckling och ett praktiskt säkerhetsarbete för hela vår verksamhet. Du agerar som Publitechs CISO och är den drivande kraften i att fortsätta bygga en säkerhetskultur i vår distribuerade organisation. Vidare kommer du att ingå i Publitechs ledningsgrupp och du kommer att leda ett engagerat och kompetent team bestående av två jurister och en Compliance Manager.

### **Dina huvudsakliga ansvarsområden**

* **Strategiskt ledarskap & kultur:** Leda enheten för Compliance, Security och Legal samt driva en företagskultur där säkerhet och compliance fortsatt ses som en stark konkurrensfördel och en integrerad del i vår vardag.
* **Säkerhetsstrategi & rådgivning (CISO-ansvar)**: Äga organisationens övergripande säkerhetsstrategi och omsätta komplexa regulatoriska krav (t.ex. NIS2) till praktiska, tekniska kontroller.
* **Risk- & incidenthantering**: Leda arbetet med att identifiera och minimera cyberrisker samt säkerställa robusta strukturer för incidentberedskap.
* **Ledningssystem & ISO**: Förvalta bolagets certifieringar (ISO 9001, 14001, 27001) och säkerställa att de fungerar som levande, effektiva verktyg i det dagliga arbetet.
* **Governance & distribuerad styrning**: Utveckla policies och verktyg som gör det enkelt för alla att göra rätt, samt tydliggöra gränssnittet mellan central kontroll och lokalt genomförande inom organisationen.
* **Leverantörsstyrning & tredjepartsrisk**: Etablera ett enhetligt ramverk för leverantörers hela livscykel, inklusive riskbedömning och löpande uppföljning av kritiska partners.
* **Pragmatiskt samarbete**: Agera brygga mellan olika avdelningar genom att översätta komplexa krav till konkreta handlingar som balanserar affärsdriv med säkerhet.

  

Tjänsten är en tillsvidareanställning och placeringsort är flexibelt mellan Stockholm, Göteborg, Malmö, Växjö, Helsingborg och Skellefteå. För rätt kandidat kan andra orter eventuellt vara aktuella. Vi tror på frihet under ansvar och tillämpar en flexibel hybridmodell där du kan kombinera arbete på kontoret med att arbeta hemifrån.  

  



### **Vem vi söker**


Vi söker en trygg och engagerad ledare med flerårig erfarenhet av att arbeta med informationssäkerhet och GRC. Du är en strategisk och affärsmässig person som kan lyfta blicken och se hur lagkrav påverkar affären på lång sikt. Du förstår att compliance är till för att möjliggöra affärer, och du har det affärsmässiga omdömet som krävs för att sätta gränser när riskerna blir för stora.


Vidare är du en skicklig kommunikatör som med enkelhet anpassar ditt budskap oavsett om du talar med utvecklare, externa partners eller bolagets styrelse. Du har förmågan att snabbt läsa av en situation, förstå motpartens utmaningar och presentera en väg framåt som adresserar både regeltolkning och operativ effektivitet. Du motiveras av att bygga välfungerande strukturer, skapa tydlighet och driva förbättringar tillsammans med ditt team.

**Vi ser även att du har**

* Dokumenterad erfarenhet av ledande arbete inom informationssäkerhet (CISO eller motsvarande) och GRC
* Teknisk förståelse för mjukvaruutveckling, molntjänster och cybersäkerhet
* Erfarenhet av att arbeta med ISO-standarder (särskilt ISO 27001) och regulatoriska krav (t.ex. GDPR, NIS2 eller liknande)
* Erfarenhet av att leda specialister
* Universitets- eller högskoleutbildning inom IT, systemvetenskap, juridik, civilingenjör, eller motsvarande kunskaper förvärvade på annat sätt
* God kommunikationsförmåga på både svenska och engelska, i tal och skrift
* En nyfiken inställning till ny teknik och använder moderna digitala verktyg, inklusive AI, för att effektivisera och förbättra ditt och enhetens arbete

  

Det är även meriterande med

* Erfarenhet från svensk offentlig sektor och god förståelse för OSL (Offentlighets- och sekretesslagen) samt säkerhetsskydd.

### **Publitech**


Hos oss på Publitech får du det bästa av två världar. Vi drar nytta av expertisen, musklerna och tryggheten i att tillhöra Visma, en av Europas ledande IT-koncerner, samtidigt som vi behåller själen hos ett mindre, entreprenöriellt bolag. Här möts du av ett nära samarbete och en varm, stöttande kultur.


Vår vision är tydlig: vi ska stärka offentlig sektor med innovativa lösningar som maximerar verksamhetsnyttan och bidrar till ett hållbart samhälle. Vi är dessutom otroligt stolta över vårt höga medarbetarengagemang som placerar oss i topp 5 % i hela tech-branschen!


I din roll och hos oss får du:

* **En flexibel arbetsplats:** Vi vet att livet inte är ”one size fits all”. Därför har du stor frihet att själv styra ditt arbete och din tid för att få en hållbar balans i vardagen.
* **Göra verklig skillnad:** Du arbetar med spännande publika kunder vars verksamheter påverkar människors liv, varje dag. Allt vi gör syftar till att förenkla och förbättra för dem.
* **En stark teamkänsla:** Du omges av engagerade kollegor i en kultur som präglas av innovation, tätt samarbete och där ni tillsammans brinner för att skapa värde för samhället.

### **Ansökan**


Du söker tjänsten via länken. Vi kommer att genomföra urval och kalla till intervjuer löpande, skicka därför in din ansökan så snart som möjligt. Har du frågor eller önskar mer information om tjänsten är du välkommen att kontakta Nina Lemic, Managing Director: nina.lemic@visma.com.
  

Varmt välkommen med din ansökan!

  

I denna rekrytering kommer bakgrundskontroller att genomföras på slutkandidat.

title: IT-lead - Hemfint Group
description:
Hemfint Group, bestående av de framgångsrika e-handelsbolagen Hemfint och Outl1 med en omsättning på cirka 500 miljoner kronor och försäljning på fyra marknader, är en del av BHG Group – en ledande aktör inom fritids-, hem- och trädgårdsprodukter. Vi erbjuder en dynamisk arbetsmiljö med fokus på tillväxt, lönsamhet och kundnöjdhet.


Nu söker vi en tekniskt driven och lösningsorienterad person som vill vara med och utveckla vår IT-miljö och våra digitala plattformar. Rollen passar dig som gillar kodning, tycker om att lösa problem och trivs med många kontaktytor i organisationen.


Hos oss får du en varierad roll där du arbetar både operativt och utvecklingsnära tillsammans med verksamheten. Du blir en viktig del av organisationen och arbetar nära lager, kundservice, marknad, inköp och försäljning för att utveckla smarta och effektiva lösningar i vardagen.

###### **Om rollen**


I rollen kommer du att arbeta med vidareutveckling och förbättring av våra egenutvecklade e-handelsplattformar och interna system. Du kommer att jobba med kravställning mot extern aktör, integrationer och tekniska förbättringar som skapar affärsnytta och hjälper verksamheten att växa.


Du arbetar även med:

* integrationer mellan e-handelssystem, affärssystem, lager- och logistiksystem samt externa tjänster,
* databaser och informationsflöden,
* teknisk felsökning och förbättringsarbete,
* drift, underhåll och optimering av våra IT-miljöer,
* säkerhetslösningar, backup och stabilitet i affärskritiska system,
* dialog och kravställning mot externa IT-leverantörer,
* projekt och tekniska förbättringsinitiativ.


Rollen innebär stor variation och passar dig som tycker om att kombinera teknik med samarbete och verksamhetsförståelse.  

För sätt person finns det på sikt goda möjligheter att växa in i en mer ansvarstagande och strategisk roll.

###### **Vi söker dig som**

* har erfarenhet inom IT, systemutveckling eller teknisk förvaltning,
* har erfarenhet av kodning och teknisk problemlösning,
* har förståelse för integrationer, databaser och systemmiljöer,
* tycker om att samarbeta med olika delar av verksamheten,
* är kommunikativ, hjälpsam och prestigelös,
* är strukturerad och lösningsorienterad.


Det är meriterande om du har erfarenhet av e-handel, logistikflöden eller affärssystem, men det viktigaste är att du har rätt driv, är tekniskt intresserad och tycker om att arbeta nära verksamheten.

###### **Vi erbjuder**


Hos oss får du en viktig roll i ett växande e-handelsbolag där du får möjlighet att påverka och utvecklas tillsammans med drivna kollegor. Vi sitter i moderna lokaler vid Hötorget i Stockholm och erbjuder en trivsam arbetsmiljö med frukost varje morgon samt tillgång till gym till ett väldigt förmånligt pris för att främja hälsa och välbefinnande.
